next_steps: Obtain permanent sections and immunohistochemistry from the surgical biopsy for CD20, CD3, GFAP, and EBER
Perform flow cytometry on cerebrospinal fluid for lymphoma cells
Test cerebrospinal fluid for Epstein-Barr virus (EBV) via PCR
Perform full-body PET/CT scan to screen for occult malignancy or systemic inflammatory disease
Conduct serological evaluation for autoimmune encephalitides and vasculitis (e.g., ANCA, ANA, ACE level)
Hold chemotherapy/radiation until definitive histopathological diagnosis is established
Continue high-dose corticosteroids while awaiting pathology results
Obtain permanent histopathology including IHC (CD20, CD3, CD79a, Ki-67, GFAP, EBER-ISH)
Perform Brain MRI with perfusion and spectroscopy to assess vascularity and metabolite patterns
Evaluate CSF for cytology, flow cytometry, protein, glucose, and PCR for EBV and JC virus
Order full-body PET/CT scan to screen for systemic lymphoma or occult malignancy
Request specialized stains on biopsy tissue for acid-fast bacilli, fungi (GMS), and bacteria (Gram/Brown-Hopps)
Coordinate with neurology/neurosurgery to manage mass effect with corticosteroids while avoiding empirical therapy before definitive diagnosis
